title: www_justice_gov_uk_courts_procedure_rules_civil_contents_frontmatter_Notes25_ad06b0be6a.html
content: NOTES TO ACCOMPANY DECEMBER 2001 25th SUPPLEMENT – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . Home   Courts Procedure rules Offenders Search Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter NOTES TO ACCOMPANY DECEMBER 2001 25th SUPPLEMENT Home » Courts » Procedure rules » Civil » contents » frontmatter » NOTES TO ACCOMPANY DECEMBER 2001 25th SUPPLEMENT Menu ≡ NOTES TO ACCOMPANY DECEMBER 2001 25th SUPPLEMENT Amendments to existing material are included as follows: Rules Part 9 Rule 9.2 cross reference to ‘Derivative Claims’ removed Practice Directions: Practice Direction 4 Existing Part 4 Practice Direction revised to cover changes to the table of forms THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 7 New Practice Direction 7E inserted Practice Direction 23 New para 6.5(1) substituted for existing para THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 49B Heading amended after ‘the Companies Act 1985’ and ‘Part VII of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000’ inserted. In para 1(1), after definition of ‘the ICA’, definition of Part VII FISMA inserted. In para 1(3), after ‘the Act’, in both places where it occurs, ‘Part VII FISMA’ is inserted. Opening words of para 2(1) substituted with new text. In para 2(4) ‘or to applications under the ICA’ deleted. In para 3, after ‘the Act’ new text inserted. After para 3, new text inserted. In para 10, after ‘the Act’, new text inserted. Practice Direction 55 Revised para 1.5 has been inserted Other Practice Directions THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Schedule 2 CCR Order 49 Practice Direction CCR Order 49 Rule 6A Practice Direction has been removed Pre-Action Protocols Judicial Review New protocol inserted Resolution of Clinical Disputes Second sentence of Section 5 deleted and replaced with new text THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction on Protocols New Judicial Review Protocol – reference inserted in table Forms Forms with ‘Embargoed until 15 October 2001’ have now had this wording removed relevant forms are: N2 (241); N2A (243); N3 (247); N5 (191); N5A 9193); N5B (195); N5C (199); N7 (201); N7A (203); N7B (205); N11 (207); N119A (229); N120 (231); N121 (235); N205D (249); N206A (237); N206B (239) Index to forms After the titles of the forms N2, N2A, N2B and N3, the words ‘probate claim’ have been added Replacement forms Forms N150, N161A and N260 replace already existing versions in the forms section, and these are already in force. In the form N150 Allocation Questionnaire, Notes for Guidance, at ‘A Settlement’, last sentence deleted and replaced with new text Coming into force Practice directions: The above Practice Direction changes come into force on 3rd December 2001, with the exception of Practice Direction 55 which came into force on 15th October 2001 and Practice Direction 7E which comes into force on 17th December 2001. Pre-Action Protocols: The Judicial Review Pre-Action Protocol comes into force on 4th March 2002. The change to the Pre-Action Protocol for the Resolution of Clinical Disputes comes into force on 3rd December 2001. Forms: The form changes come into force immediately.
